allow.description: Defines the list of allowed protocols and ports for the firewall rule, specifying which traffic is permitted.

deny.description: Defines the list of deny rules specifying protocols and ports to block in the firewall configuration.

description.description: Specifies the purpose or details of the firewall rule; can be up to 2048 characters.

destination_ranges.description: Specifies the list of destination IP address ranges in CIDR format for the firewall rule; used to define traffic targets.

direction.description: Specifies the traffic direction for the firewall rule; must be either "INGRESS" for incoming traffic or "EGRESS" for outgoing traffic.

disabled.description: Indicates whether the firewall rule is disabled; set to true to disable the rule and prevent it from being applied.

enable_logging.description: Determines whether logging is enabled for the firewall rule; requires logging to be supported in the project.

log_config.description: Configures logging options for the firewall rule, including whether to enable logging and the type of log metadata captured.

name.description: Unique name for the firewall rule, used to identify it within the project.

network.description: Specifies the VPC network where the firewall rule will be applied. Must be the name of an existing VPC network in the same project.

priority.description: Defines the priority of the firewall rule, determining the order in which rules are evaluated. Lower values indicate higher priority. Must be an integer between 0 and 65535.

source_ranges.description: Defines the IP address ranges that are allowed to make inbound connections; must be specified in CIDR format.

source_service_accounts.description: Specifies the service accounts allowed to send traffic through the firewall rule, must be formatted as email addresses.

source_tags.description: Defines the network tags that a source instance must have to match this firewall rule.

target_service_accounts.description: Specifies the list of service accounts that are allowed as targets for the firewall rule; must be valid and existing service accounts within the project.

target_tags.description: Specifies the network tags of instances to which the firewall rule will apply; must match the tags assigned to the target instances.

timeouts.description: Defines the amount of time to wait for the creation, update, or deletion of the firewall rule to complete.
